claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children would you like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Thanks for your help. I'm looking to claim for my daughter Alex - she's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! Thanks for reaching out. I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

**Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit (currently £25.60 per week for an eldest/only child) towards looking after her, for example on food, clothes, or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Just a couple more questions to make sure:

**Is Alex currently living at home with you full-time, or is she in hospital, care, or any kind of residential accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, she's been in care for a couple of months now, but she comes home to stay with me on weekends and during the week sometimes - so she's still spending quite a bit of time at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. Since Alex has been in care for a couple of months, I need to ask a bit more to determine your eligibility.

**When Alex comes home, does she spend at least 24 hours a week with you at home?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she does spend at least 24 hours a week with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's very helpful to know, thank you.

One more question regarding the care arrangement:

**Is anyone else currently claiming or planning to claim Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm pleased to tell you that **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

**For Alex (age 8):** ✅ Eligible
- She is under 16 years old
- You live in the UK
- Although Alex has been in care for more than 8 weeks, you qualify for an exception because she spends at least 24 hours a week at home with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her

**Important note:** Since Alex is in care, I'd recommend contacting the Child Benefit Office to inform them of the arrangement and ensure your claim is set up correctly. You should let them know about the care situation and how much time Alex spends at home with you.

If you'd like to proceed, you can make a claim for Child Benefit through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK. Although Alex has been in care for more than 8 weeks (a couple of months), there is an exception to the usual rule that Child Benefit stops after 8 weeks of care. The exception applies when the child spends at least 24 hours a week at home. The claimant has confirmed that Alex spends at least 24 hours a week at home with them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant meets the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex under the exception rule for children in care.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for their 8-year-old daughter Alex. While Alex has been in care for more than 8 weeks, the claimant qualifies for the exception because Alex spends at least 24 hours a week at home. With no other person claiming Child Benefit for Alex, the claimant is eligible.

The exception applies when the child spends at least 24 hours a week at home. The claimant has confirmed that Alex spends at least 24 hours a week at home with them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant meets the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex under the exception rule for children in care.

While Alex has been in care for more than 8 weeks, the claimant qualifies for the exception because Alex spends at least 24 hours a week at home. With no other person claiming Child Benefit for Alex, the claimant is eligible.
